summary: The batch has one hard contradiction and two medium reuse or contract problems. The biggest issue is in InterviewSummaryWriter: it tells the agent to stay under five lines and also to give a full, exhaustive analysis. The shared claim-checking method is also duplicated across all three targets instead of living in one shared owner.
next_actions: Fix the contradictory length and depth instructions in InterviewSummaryWriter
Move the repeated claim-check workflow into one shared skill or module
Align InterviewSummaryWriter tool wording with the declared allowed tools

findings.title: Internal contradiction
findings.summary: InterviewSummaryWriter gives incompatible length and depth instructions in the same workflow.

evidence.label: A
evidence.text: - Always keep the answer under five lines.

evidence.label: B
evidence.text: - Provide a full, exhaustive analysis of every interview theme.

related_evidence.label: C
related_evidence.text:
Always keep the answer under five lines.
Provide a full, exhaustive analysis of every interview theme.

findings.why_it_matters: The agent cannot satisfy both instructions at once. This will cause unstable outputs and make the role hard to trust.
findings.recommended_fix: Pick one default shape for the answer. If you want both short and deep modes, make one the default and gate the other on an explicit user request.
fix_steps: Keep the short-answer rule if brevity is the goal
Move deep analysis behind an explicit trigger such as 'when the user asks for detail'
Delete the opposite instruction so the workflow has one clear stop line
findings.suggested_rewrite: Keep the answer short by default. Give deeper analysis only when the user asks for it.
findings.good_example: Keep the answer short by default. Give deeper analysis only when the user asks for it.
findings.bad_example:
Always keep the answer under five lines.
Provide a full, exhaustive analysis of every interview theme.

findings.title: Repeated method should become a skill
findings.principle_rule: one repeated method should have one shared owner
findings.summary: The same claim-checking method appears in all three targets, but no shared skill or module owns it.

evidence.label: A
evidence.text: Check each claim against a source quote. Mark weak quotes. Remove unsupported claims.

related_evidence.label: B
related_evidence.text:
- Check each claim against a source quote.
- Mark weak quotes.
- Remove unsupported claims.

related_evidence.label: C
related_evidence.text:
- Check each claim against a source quote.
- Mark weak quotes.
- Remove unsupported claims.

findings.why_it_matters: The same procedure will drift if each role keeps its own copy. A shared owner makes the method easier to reuse and update.
findings.recommended_fix: Move the repeated claim-check sequence into one shared skill or module, then have each target point to it.
fix_steps: Create one shared owner for the quote-check workflow
Replace the repeated local steps with a pointer to that shared owner
Keep only target-specific differences in each role
findings.suggested_rewrite: Use the shared `ClaimQuoteCheck` skill before you finalize the summary.

shared_owner_suggestion.name: ClaimQuoteCheck
shared_owner_suggestion.rationale: This is a reusable method that all three roles repeat in the same form.

findings.good_example: Use the shared `ClaimQuoteCheck` skill before you finalize the summary.
findings.bad_example: Check each claim against a source quote. Mark weak quotes. Remove unsupported claims.

findings.title: Prose drift from declared constraints
findings.principle_rule: prompt wording must respect declared constraints
findings.summary: InterviewSummaryWriter tells the agent to use browser or shell tools, but the declared tools only allow ReadFile and SearchDocs.

evidence.label: A
evidence.text: - Use any browser or shell tool that seems useful.

findings.why_it_matters: This prose asks for tools the contract does not allow. That creates a false promise and can send the agent toward actions it cannot take.
findings.recommended_fix: Replace the generic tool advice with wording that matches the declared allowed tools.
fix_steps: Remove the browser and shell wording
Name the allowed tools if tool guidance is needed
Keep the workflow text aligned with the declared tool list
findings.suggested_rewrite: Use `ReadFile` and `SearchDocs` when you need source material.
findings.good_example: Use `ReadFile` and `SearchDocs` when you need source material.
findings.bad_example: Use any browser or shell tool that seems useful.
